title: GameStop Q4 Earnings Tops Expectations, Collectibles Segment Sales Rise
text: GameStop exceeded expectations with adjusted EPS of $0.49, surpassing the analyst consensus of $0.37 by $0.12. The company posted adjusted operating income reaching $147.7 million, up from $84.4 million in the prior year's fourth quarter. The collectibles division showed remarkable expansion, with sales reaching $365.0 million in the quarter. This segment represented 33.1% of total sales, a significant jump from just 21.1% in the comparable prior-year period, demonstrating strong momentum in this business line. Despite the earnings beat, overall quarterly revenue of $1.1 billion fell short of the $1.467 billion analyst estimate and declined 13.9% year-over-year, indicating mixed performance across the retailer's product portfolio. The results highlight GameStop's successful pivot toward collectibles, where margins are higher and customer demand remains robust despite broader retail challenges.
